content.problemStatement:
Chef is exploring pointers in C. Chef has an array of integers and wants to print its elements starting from the last element back to the first.

Your task is to help Chef to print the array in reverse using a pointer.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer `T`, the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer `n`, the number of elements in the array.
The second line contains `n` space separated integers.


Output Format
For each test case, print the `n` integers in reverse order separated by a space.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains n space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the n integers in reverse order separated by a single space. Print a newline after each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case array [1,2,3,4,5] reversed is [5,4,3,2,1]. Second test case array [10,20,30] reversed is [30,20,10].
A single element array remains the same when reversed.
Reversing the ten‑element array yields the numbers from 10 down to 1.

solution.approach: The task is to output the given array in reverse order. The simplest way is to read the whole array into a list, then iterate from the last index down to zero while printing each element. This requires O(n) time per test case and O(n) auxiliary space for storing the array. No special data structures are needed; a plain array or vector suffices. The algorithm works because reversing a sequence is just a linear scan from the end.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) per test case for storing the array.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out_lines=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        n=int(next(it))
        arr=[int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str,arr[::-1])))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
